subject: PhD Analysis
instructions: Problems 1–12: State the following theorems. Prove one of the theorems in problems 8 or 9. Problems 13–18: Solve the following problems. \((X,\Sigma,\mu)\) is a measure space, \(E\) is a Banach space.

problems.text: The Riesz representation theorem in locally compact spaces

Let \((f_n)\) be a sequence of \(L_E^1(\mu)\) such that \(\int |f_n|\,d\mu < \frac{1}{n^2}\) for every \(n\). Prove or disprove the pointwise convergence and the convergence in the mean of the series \(\sum f_n\).

Assume \(\mu\) is finite and let \(R\) be a ring generating \(\Sigma\). Define \(\rho(A,B)=\mu(A\mathbin{\triangle}B)\) for \(A,B\in\Sigma\). Prove that \(\rho\) is a semi-distance and that \(R\) is dense in \(\Sigma\) for \(\rho\).

Let \(X=\mathbb{R}\) and \(\mu\) the Lebesgue measure. Let \(f\in L_E^1(\mu)\) such that \(\int_0^x f\,d\mu=0\) for every \(x\in\mathbb{R}\) (if \(x<0\), \(\int_0^x=-\int_x^0\)). Prove that \(f=0\), \(\mu\)-a.e.

Let \(\mu,
u\) be finite, \(\sigma\)-additive measures on \(\Sigma\), such that \(\mu\ll
u\) and \(
u\ll\mu\). What can be said about the relationship of the Radon–Nikodym densities of one measure with respect to the other.

Let \((x_n)\) be a sequence in the Banach space \(E\). Assume \(x_n\to x\) weakly in \(E\). Show that \(x\) is the only weak limit of the sequence \((x_n)\) and that \(\sup_n\lVert x_n\rVert<\infty\). (Hint: Consider the \(x_n\) as elements of the bidual \(E^{**}\).)

Let \(X=\mathbb{R}\) and \(\mu\) the Lebesgue measure. Let \(f\in L^1(\mu)\) and for each \(t\in\mathbb{R}\) define \(f_t(x)=f(x-t)\). Show that the mapping \(t\mapsto f_t\) is a continuous map from \(\mathbb{R}\) into \(L^1(\mu)\). (Hint: prove it first for continuous functions with compact support).
